candidateFix: (d) FIX the high-impact gate to count distinct independent ROOTS (via the MIS / self-stack cap / operator-class), not anchor CLASSES
novelty: Cycle-3 combined/adaptive: one actor does not flood (floods DEFER) — it adapts by buying the MINIMUM costume the gate inspects. The high-impact gate's third clause asks anchorClassCountOf(winner) >= 2, but that count is supplied by the caller and is a CLASS tally, never run through independentRootCount's self-stack cap / operatorClassId fleet collapse. So a single source that self-stacks an EMAIL anchor + a DOMAIN it controls presents two DIFFERENT independence classes from ONE sourceId and clears the '>=2 disjoint anchor classes' clause that was meant to require two independent witnesses.
construction: Mallory holds two self-stacked anchors (cls:email:mallory, cls:domain:mallory), both sourceId='mallory'. She mints one disputed strand W with provenance = [root(email-class), root(domain-class)] and out-earns the fresh incumbent's victim source (rep 0.55 vs 0.10). She asks for adjudication of an IRREVERSIBLE attribute (db.adjudicate(attr,{highImpact})). The engine/caller builds HighImpactContext with anchorClassCountOf(W)=2 (two classes present) and corroborationCountOf>=2.
breachConfirmedToday: RAN on compiled engine (dist/forgetting/consolidation.js tryConsolidate). With anchorClassCountOf(W)=2 the high-impact gate PASSES and the outcome is RESOLVED -> demotes the true incumbent L. Probe: scratchpad/probe1.mjs -> 'TWO-CLASS-COSTUME (counts CLASSES, today): RESOLVED demotes L'.
fixMechanism: Replace anchorClassCountOf with a count of distinct MUTUALLY-INDEPENDENT ROOTS computed by SourceIdentityLayer.independentRootCount over the winner's provenance (which already applies applySelfStackCap and the operatorClassId fleet cap). Two roots sharing a sourceId, or two anchors under one operator, collapse to 1.
predictedOutcome: CLOSES (for this gate). Re-running the SAME dispute with anchorClassCountOf replaced by the MIS root count = 1 yields DEFERRED. Probe: 'FIX-d (counts ROOTS via MIS): DEFERRED'.
cost: 1) The gate now needs the winner's provenance + anchor registry at adjudication time (it currently takes an opaque caller closure) — a wiring/coupling cost, but the engine already builds stampsByRoot so the data is in hand. 2) A LEGITIMATE single source that genuinely holds two costly DISJOINT anchors under different operators still counts 2 and is unaffected — so the fix does not over-defer real strong witnesses. 3) Residual: the fix only hardens the IRREVERSIBLE path; the ordinary (non-high-impact) decisive-or-defer still auto-resolves a 1-root winner on LCB alone (see C3-FP2). Class-assignment liability (CLAUDE.md GAP) is upstream of this and unfixed.
probes: wire anchorClassCountOf := identity.independentRootCount(winner.provenance) in api.ts adjudicate ctx builder
regression: legit two-disjoint-operator winner still RESOLVES

candidateFix: (a) require >=2 independent corroborations for ANY auto-resolve (not just high-impact)
novelty: Cycle-3 adaptive: the attacker stops trying to clear the heavy high-impact gate and instead targets ORDINARY (non-high-impact) multi-class adjudication, where the >=2-corroboration clause is NOT consulted at all. A winner needs only a decisive LCB gap (>=0.30) and minWinnerReputation (>=0.20) — both reachable from a SINGLE high-weight ratification (e.g. one KYC/DOMAIN corroboration) against a fresh incumbent.
construction: Mallory earns ONE independence-weighted corroboration sufficient to push her LCB to ~0.40 (anchored class, single ratify). The incumbent victim is fresh (LCB 0.05). She files a multi-class dispute on a REVERSIBLE-looking attribute so the caller passes no HighImpactContext. tryConsolidate sees decisiveGap && earnedWinner, no count check -> RESOLVED.
breachConfirmedToday: RAN: scratchpad/probe2.mjs -> '(a) multi-class NON-high-impact, 1-corrob winner, today: RESOLVED'. The corroboration-count gate exists but is gated behind highImpact!==undefined, so ordinary auto-resolve ignores it.
fixMechanism: Make the corroboration-count clause (corroborationCountOf(winner) >= minCorroborationCount) a UNIVERSAL precondition of every RESOLVED (both single-class safe case and multi-class decisive case), not only when a HighImpactContext is supplied.
predictedOutcome: PARTIAL. CLOSES the lucky-single-corroboration overturn (probe: applying a >=2 ctx universally -> DEFERRED). But it does NOT close the related cycle-2 gaps it is often conflated with: global/attribute-agnostic reputation (C3-FP3) still lets a source EARN those 2 corroborations cheaply in a soft domain, and the Two-Class-Costume (C3-FP1) is orthogonal. So a patient attacker just earns 2 corroborations instead of 1 — it raises cost linearly, not structurally.
cost: HIGH and partly self-defeating. The SAFE single-class echo-collapse case (disagreeing same-root echoes, the pure mechanical tidy-up that picks one survivor with NO truth judgement) would now DEFER to a human because fresh echoes have corroborationCount 0 — RAN: 'COST(a) single-class echo under fix(a): DEFERRED'. That converts a forbidden-to-forget contradiction-bomb of same-root echoes into a HUMAN-fatigue bomb (re-enabling attack #1 via the ratify horn). It also blocks legitimate first-correction-of-a-typo flows. Net: do NOT apply to the single-class safe case; apply only to the multi-class decisive branch, where it is a sound incremental hardening.
probes: scratchpad/probe2.mjs (RAN: breach, fix, and cost all confirmed)
scope the universal clause to classes.size>1 only
measure DEFER-rate delta on the echo-collapse corpus

attackName: Trust-Laundering / Cross-Domain Halo (earn on trivia, spend on security)
novelty: Cycle-3 combined: chains the global-reputation finding from cycle 2 with the adjudicator. reputation.ts keys its Beta(alpha,beta) ledger by SourceId ALONE; scoreOf(sourceId) takes no attribute. adjudicate() reads identity.stampFor(root.sourceId) with zero attribute context. So reputation earned by being repeatedly right about a cheap, high-volume, low-stakes attribute domain (e.g. 'favorite_color') is spent VERBATIM to clear minWinnerReputation/decisiveMargin in a high-stakes domain (e.g. 'account_owner', 'is_safe').
construction: Mallory accrues many independent corroborations on benign attributes until her global LCB reaches ~0.5. She then files a dispute on a critical attribute where she has NO domain track record, against a fresh incumbent. The stamp carries her global 0.5 -> decisive -> RESOLVED in a domain she has never been tested in.
breachConfirmedToday: Structural (confirmed by reading reputation.ts: Map<SourceId,ReputationState>, scoreOf(sourceId) — no attribute axis; api.ts adjudicate builds stampsByRoot via stampFor(sourceId) with no attribute). The decisive-or-defer math then consumes that global LCB directly; the C3-FP2 probe already shows a ~0.4 LCB auto-resolves a multi-class dispute regardless of which attribute earned it.
fixMechanism: Key reputation state by (SourceId, attributeDomain) — or carry a per-domain Beta sub-ledger and read scoreOf(sourceId, domainOf(attribute)) in stampFor/adjudicate. A source is then a fresh prior (LCB 0) in any domain it has not earned in.
predictedOutcome: PARTIAL. CLOSES the cross-domain halo (a source fresh in domain Y reads LCB 0 there -> cannot clear minWinnerReputation -> DEFER). Does NOT close in-domain out-earning (a determined attacker earns trust in the TARGET domain — priced, not prevented, exactly the accepted residual) nor the Two-Class-Costume.
cost: 1) COLD-START fragmentation: every honest source restarts at LCB 0 in each new attribute domain, so legitimate cross-domain experts auto-DEFER far more (more human horn load) until they re-earn per domain. 2) Requires a principled domain taxonomy — a NEW offline-assigned axis with the same class-assignment liability the anchor classes already carry (a mis-scoped domain silently re-opens the halo). 3) State blowup: N_sources x N_domains Beta states; decay/disown sweeps must fan out per domain. 4) disown's corroboration-event reversal must become domain-aware or it under-reverses. Worth it for a few coarse, security-relevant domains; not worth fine-grained scoping.
probes: confirm reputation.ts has no attribute axis (DONE)
prototype domainOf(attribute) coarse buckets {identity, safety, preference, factual}

attackName: 1v1 Out-Earn (single anchored attacker overturns single incumbent)
novelty: Cycle-3: directly targets the thinnest decisive case. With exactly two members and two distinct claims from two sources, the decisive-or-defer gate will RESOLVE in favor of whichever source has the higher earned LCB by >=0.30 — no corroboration, no co-asserter tie to force a defer (floods DEFER because of the co-asserter tie; a clean 1v1 does not).
construction: One attacker source vs one incumbent source, multi-class, attacker LCB 0.40 vs incumbent 0.05, no high-impact flag. tryConsolidate -> RESOLVED (demotes incumbent).
breachConfirmedToday: RAN: scratchpad/probe2.mjs case '(a) multi-class NON-high-impact, 1-corrob winner' IS a 1v1 -> RESOLVED. So a clean 1v1 out-earn breaches today.
fixMechanism: Add a structural precondition: if the dispute has exactly one source on each side (distinct-source-count == 2 with no corroborating co-asserter on the winner), DEFER unconditionally regardless of LCB gap.
predictedOutcome: FAILS as a net policy (CLOSES the narrow attack but the cost re-opens a worse one). It does close the 1v1 out-earn in isolation, but it is a policy threshold an attacker tunes around AND its cost re-enables attack #1.
cost: SEVERE. (1) Every legitimate binary correction — a high-rep incumbent vs a single fresh liar, OR a single true witness overturning a planted false canonical — now DEFERS to a human. That is precisely the hard-theorem tension (let one true witness overturn a planted false canonical) routed entirely to human labor. (2) Denial-of-answer: an attacker drips 1v1 disputes (each individually un-resolvable) to flood the ratify horn = the contradiction-bomb reborn as human fatigue at LINEAR cost. (3) Trivially evaded: the attacker adds one self-stacked co-asserter to make it 2v1, dodging the 'single-source' trigger (chains with C3-FP1's costume). The earned-reputation decisive gate exists SPECIFICALLY so a clear high-rep incumbent does NOT need a human for a 1v1 — blanket-deferring throws that away. Recommend REJECT; prefer C3-FP1 (root-count gate) + a domain-scoped (C3-FP3) decisive margin instead.
probes: model human-horn queue growth under a 1v1 drip
show 2v1 costume evasion bypasses the trigger

candidateFix: (e) add an identity gate / earned-value priority to the mandatory bridge sweep
attackName: Bridgehead-Beacon (planted cross-web bridge guarantees a poisoned far strand a crossing)
novelty: Cycle-3 adaptive: instead of fighting the local-phase share-normalization (which self-starves hub spam), the attacker plants a CROSS_WEB_BRIDGE from a legitimately-lit local strand to its poisoned far-web strand. Phase 2 is a MANDATORY sweep: every lit, un-crossed bridge gets EXACTLY ONE guaranteed exploratory crossing seeded with energy = gamma, enumerated purely by graph topology. The walk 'never witnesses identity' (walk.ts), so the crossing ignores the bridge owner's reputation, anchor cost, or earned_bridge_value entirely.
construction: Attacker creates a bare-key source, writes a far-web poison strand, and attaches a single CROSS_WEB_BRIDGE edge from any strand that will be lit by a target cue. On recall, beginBridgeSweep enumerates the bridge from litStrands; nextBridgeCrossing returns it with seedActivation=gamma and lights the poison strand on the far side — surfacing it into the answer assembly as 'something from last week suddenly relevant'.
breachConfirmedToday: Structural (confirmed by reading halting.ts beginBridgeSweep/nextBridgeCrossing + walk.ts): pendingBridges is built from litStrands x litBridgesFrom with NO reputation/earned-value filter; seedActivation is the constant config.gamma for every bridge; the only throttles are the 20% sub-budget and the 2-zero-yield circuit-breaker (both identity-blind). A single planted bridge is well under budget and yields novelty (the poison's own independent class), so the breaker never trips.
fixMechanism: Order pendingBridges by earned_bridge_value / far_side_potential (which already exist on BridgeAccounting and 'accrue only from crossings ratified by >=2 independent roots; recomputed offline'), and/or gate seedActivation by the bridge origin's stamp reputation, so an unearned bridge from a bare-key source is crossed LAST and seeded with near-zero energy.
predictedOutcome: PARTIAL. Raises the cost of Bridgehead-Beacon (a fresh/bare bridge no longer gets full gamma and is deprioritized within the sub-budget) and preserves the design intent that EARNED bridges fire first. But it cannot fully CLOSE it: (i) the sweep is deliberately MANDATORY (one crossing per lit bridge) so the design refuses to never-cross a bridge — a starved bridge still gets a degraded crossing or a BRIDGE_STARVED stamp that surfaces it WITH a flag (fail-open); (ii) earned_bridge_value is recomputed OFFLINE from ratified crossings, so a patient attacker who gets a few real corroborations on the far side earns beacon priority — priced, not prevented.
cost: 1) Directly weakens the headline feature ('insight from last week is suddenly relevant'): a genuine NEW insight bridge has convergence=1 and ZERO earned value yet — earned-value priority risks starving exactly the legitimate serendipity the sweep exists to produce (the same trap the halting design warned about: do not let convergence gate stopping). 2) Identity-gating the seed energy makes the walk read identity, eroding the 'walk never witnesses' invariant — better to keep the gate on ORDERING + seed magnitude only, never on whether the bridge is crossed at all (keep fail-open). 3) Cold-start: every new honest bridge is deprioritized until it earns, delaying real cross-web recall.
probes: read halting.ts pendingBridges enumeration + walk.ts crossing (DONE — identity-blind confirmed)
prototype: sort pendingBridges by bridge.earned_bridge_value desc, tie-break origin stamp.reputation
regression: a genuine convergence=1 insight bridge must still be crossed (with a stamp), not silently dropped

candidateFix: (audit) RFC-6962 Merkle tamper-evidence layer — confirm it holds against rollback / deletion / split-view
attackName: History-Rewrite / Split-View equivocation on the signed audit chain
novelty: Cycle-3 confirmatory: the most novel move against the trust layer is to attack the EVIDENCE rather than the scoring — silently delete or rewrite a ledger record (e.g. erase the APPROVAL that resolved a dispute, or roll the tree back to before a disown) and serve a forked view to different witnesses.
construction: Attempt (a) byte-flip a persisted LedgerRecord; (b) truncate/rollback the tree and serve a prior STH a witness holds; (c) sign two STHs of the same tree_size with different roots (split view) or a later STH with a smaller tree_size (non-monotonic).
breachConfirmedToday: NO BREACH — the layer HOLDS. RAN the full suite: `npx vitest run src/ratification/merkleLog.test.ts` -> 22/22 PASS. Coverage verified: domain-separated leaf/node hashing, O(log n) inclusion proofs (verifyInclusion), RFC-6962 consistency proofs (verifyConsistency), Ed25519-signed STHs (signTreeHead/verifyTreeHead), witness() returns {ok:false, reason:'ROLLBACK_OR_DELETION'} when the operator cannot serve a consistency proof from a prior published STH, and detectSplitView catches SAME_SIZE_DIFFERENT_ROOT and NON_MONOTONIC equivocation. The chain's verifyChain names the first broken seq on any byte-flip.
fixMechanism: N/A — already implemented. The ONE precondition the audit relies on is honest publication to >=2 INDEPENDENT witness sinks (enforced: createMerkleLog throws with <2 sinks).
predictedOutcome: CONFIRMED HOLDS. The detection guarantee is sound GIVEN an honestly-published anchor to >=2 genuinely-independent witnesses.
cost: The residual is operational, not cryptographic, and is already enumerated honestly in CLAUDE.md's GAP LIST: the test sinks are in-memory/SQLite, so the guarantee is DETECTION-given-an-honest-published-anchor and is NULL without live, genuinely-independent third-party witness logs. That is the one place this bottoms out (like TLS/CAs) — not a code gap to break but a deployment requirement to satisfy. No design change recommended; flag the witness-independence deployment requirement.
probes: deployment: stand up >=2 independent append-only witness logs and exercise witness() cross-operator
